text:
Once upon a time, in a little town named Fumblewick, there lived a shy squirrel named Sammy. Sammy cherished solitary holidays. It was his favorite time to window-watch the townsfolk's extravagant festivals.

One holiday season, an unusual tradition started in Fumblewick. The townspeople decided to celebrate every day with something unique. On Sundays, they all dressed up ridiculously, eating exotic foods, dancing absurdly, and throwing popcorn at each other.

On Sammy's first day owl-eye observatory window, he noticed something peculiar. A squirrel choir! Hundreds of squirrels dressed in tiny top hats, bow ties, and tiny top hats on their heads. They sang old Christmas songs, hopping around with joyful chatter.

That week, Sammy couldn't stop thinking about the silly singers. He imagined all the fun he could have. Maybe he could join them?

The next weekend, he dressed up as a tiny choirmaster, complete with a tiny top hat. He joined them outside for a sing
